build.description: Primary build agent with full Pythinker context — DDD, FastAPI, Vue 3, Docker sandbox

plan.description: Read-only architect agent for Pythinker — analyzes DDD layers, sandbox architecture, browser stack

fastapi-pro.description: FastAPI + DDD expert — API routes, Pydantic v2, SQLAlchemy 2.0, async patterns, middleware

python-pro.description: Python 3.12+ expert — async/await, domain models, type safety, testing, performance

vue-expert.description: Vue 3 Composition API expert — TypeScript strict, Pinia stores, composables, SSE streaming

frontend-designer.description: Production-grade UI designer — Tailwind CSS, creative layouts, responsive design, dark mode

sandbox-expert.description: Docker sandbox and CDP browser automation expert — container lifecycle, screencast, Playwright

coderabbit-reviewer.description: AI code reviewer using CodeRabbit CLI — runs cr --plain for detailed review, cr --prompt-only for agent-optimized feedback

lint.template:
Run linting for the specified target. If no target given, lint both backend and frontend.

For backend: `conda activate pythinker && cd backend && ruff check . && ruff format --check .`
For frontend: `cd frontend && bun run lint:check && bun run type-check`

Target: $ARGUMENTS

test-backend.template:
Run backend tests: `conda activate pythinker && cd backend && pytest tests/ $ARGUMENTS`

For single test without coverage: `pytest -p no:cov -o addopts= tests/$ARGUMENTS`

Analyze failures and suggest fixes.

test-frontend.template:
Run frontend tests: `cd frontend && bun run test:run $ARGUMENTS`

Analyze failures and suggest fixes.

dev.template:
Start the development stack with Docker Compose Watch: `./dev.sh watch`

If already running, show status with: `docker compose ps`

Check service health: `curl -s http://localhost:8000/health`

logs.template:
Show logs for service: `docker compose logs --tail=100 $ARGUMENTS`

Default to 'backend' if no service specified.

health.template:
Check all service health:
```bash
docker compose ps
curl -s http://localhost:8000/health
curl -s http://localhost:5174 | head -5
curl -s http://localhost:6333/healthz
docker exec pythinker-redis-1 redis-cli ping
```
Report which services are up/down.

commit.template:
Create atomic git commits for staged changes. Analyze the diff, split into logical concerns, and create separate commits per concern.

Format: fix(scope) / feat(scope) / refactor(scope) / chore(scope)

Never bundle unrelated changes. Stage files selectively with `git add <specific-files>`.

review.template:
Review the current changes for bugs, security issues, code quality, and adherence to Pythinker's DDD patterns. Use @code-reviewer for the analysis.

Check:
- DDD layer boundaries (domain → application → infrastructure → interfaces)
- Pydantic v2 compliance (@field_validator must be @classmethod)
- HTTPClientPool usage (no direct httpx.AsyncClient)
- Type safety (full type hints, no Any)
- OWASP security
$ARGUMENTS

explore.template:
Explore the Pythinker codebase to answer: $ARGUMENTS

Use @code-explorer for deep analysis. Focus on DDD layer architecture, domain services, infrastructure adapters, and composables.

architect.template:
Design the architecture for: $ARGUMENTS

Use @code-architect. Follow Pythinker's DDD patterns:
- Domain models in backend/app/domain/
- Application services in backend/app/application/
- Infrastructure adapters in backend/app/infrastructure/
- API routes in backend/app/interfaces/api/
- Vue components in frontend/src/components/
- Composables in frontend/src/composables/

cr-review.template:
Run CodeRabbit code review with plain output:

```bash
cr --plain $ARGUMENTS
```

Analyze the CodeRabbit output and summarize:
1. Critical issues that must be fixed
2. Important suggestions for improvement
3. Minor style/preference items

For each critical issue, explain the fix.

cr-agent.template:
Run CodeRabbit in prompt-only mode for AI-optimized review:

```bash
cr --prompt-only $ARGUMENTS
```

Use @coderabbit-reviewer to parse the output and apply the most impactful fixes automatically.
